title: campanula thyrsoides subsp. carniolica
intro: Campanula thyrsoides subsp. carniolica, also known as Alpine bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, primarily for its expectorant properties, which help relieve congestion and coughs associated with respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties have been found to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, providing various health benefits such as alleviating symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and sore throats. Alpine bellflower can be prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
